LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF UNLAWFUL DETAINER JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT CONTINUED FROM JUL-15-2002 TO LAW AND MOTION AT JUL-17-2002, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 301. MOVING AND OPPOSING PARTIES APPEARED. MS. BOLDEN REPRESENTED IN OPEN COURT THAT THERE IS AN AMENDED STIPULATION FOR AN ENTRY OF JUDGMENT. HOWEVER, THE COURT UPON EXAMINING THE DOCUMENT NOTICED THAT MR. BLACKMON'S ATTORNEY MR. JANOWSKI HAS NOT SIGNED OFF ON THE STIPULATION. THEREFORE, MATTER ORDERED CONTINUED TO JULY 17, 2002. HON. JAMES J. MCBRIDE REPORTER: JUDITH THOMSEN, CSR 5591
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUL-17-2002 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF UNLAWFUL DETAINER JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT. MOVING AND OPPOSING PARTIES APPEARED. A R G U E D AND THE COURT REJECTED THE AMENDED JUDGMENT. ORIGINAL JUDGMENT STANDS. HON. JAMES J. MCBRIDE. REPORTER: ERIC THRONE CSR# 7855

MINI MINUTES: Settlement Conference: Dept. 210. Judge: Thomas J. Mellon, Jr.; Clerk: Dan MacDuff. APPEARANCES: Plaintiff by Mary Avery, with counsel, Judondi Bolden; defendant in pro per. The case is settled pursuant to written stipulation and Order of the Court. The trial date is vacated.
